title: P0 child: isolate Hetzner-Supabase 502 and add safe worker-only restart path

sha_binding.verified_source_note: This is PR #1258's OWN pre-merge implementation SHA -- NOT the same fact as the already-merged PR #1256 (see prior_merged_history below). #1258 adds: (1) two diagnostic gap fixes to ops-network-diagnose.yml, and (2) a new manual-dispatch-only ops-worker-recovery.yml, hardened per PM_VERDICT: CHANGES_REQUIRED on the prior head (34595446) to require an actual bounded-window 502/Bad-Gateway signature (not any claim_next_outbox failure), propagate restart failure, and verify a single clean restart transition before reporting success.

prior_merged_history.note: PR #1256 (read-only diagnostic only) already merged to main BEFORE this PR existed. This is a frozen, already-shipped fact (GitHub main is rank-1 truth) -- it is NOT part of #1258's own pre-merge verification and must not be conflated with it.

owner_boundary.pm_verdict_v1: present_and_consumed -- fresh Griff-authored PM_VERDICT: APPROVED posted 2026-07-19T07:57:24Z on PR #1256, exact head 6f142c9483823c969b52e446b8f4824cdaad6553 (the merged head), explicitly scoping approval to the workflow-dispatch read-only diagnostic and its lane/proof metadata only -- not authorizing restart/deploy/env mutation/target remap/queue replay/DB write

post_merge_findings.dispatched_run: ops-network-diagnose.yml, dispatched against main after PR #1256 merged
post_merge_findings.summary: Host DNS, container DNS, IPv4 REST GET /rest/v1/ (HTTP/2 401, cloudflare, cf-ray present), TLS handshake, Docker network state, and route/MTU/firewall all healthy. IPv6 REST unavailable only because no AAAA record exists for the domain (not a fault). Container curl probe and DB/pooler TCP test were both gaps in the original workflow -- fixed in PR #1258 (see acceptance_criteria). Worker log tail shows a real Cloudflare 502 HTML error page and a separate 'canceling statement due to statement timeout' error on the claim_next_outbox RPC path. Bounded conclusion: intermittent Cloudflare-edge/PostgREST RPC-path failure, not a Hetzner-side DNS/TLS/routing/firewall/Docker fault -- every host-level network primitive checked out healthy at diagnostic time.

verifier.note: Mechanical checks only. Not independent Griff review, t1-approved, or a pm-verdict/v1 owner artifact -- a fresh exact-head PM_VERDICT is required for PR #1258's own head before merge.

r_level.command: pnpm exec tsx scripts/ci/r-level-check.ts --base origin/main --head HEAD

workflow_safety_review.note: Self-review of both workflow files against the issue's explicit guardrails (do not mutate queues/delivery targets/env; never print secret values; recovery workflow may only restart unit-talk-worker-1 and only under a verified condition):

checks.check: ops-network-diagnose.yml: only SSH commands executed are read-only
ops-worker-recovery.yml: the ONLY mutating command in the entire file is a single `docker restart unit-talk-worker-1`; no compose up, no image pull, no env/target write

ops-worker-recovery.yml: restart predicate requires claim_next_outbox failed CO-LOCATED with an actual 502/Bad-Gateway signature on the same log line, within a 15-minute time window -- a generic claim_next_outbox failure (e.g. statement-timeout) does NOT satisfy the predicate
ops-worker-recovery.yml: docker restart's own exit code is captured and marks the run FAILED if nonzero

ops-worker-recovery.yml: post-restart verification requires status=running, image unchanged, configured target unchanged, and RestartCount advanced by exactly 1 -- any single failure marks FAILED and the step/job exits nonzero (no `set +e` suppressing the final exit code)
Secret values are never echoed anywhere in either workflow
Both workflows are workflow_dispatch-only -- no automatic/scheduled trigger, no trigger on push/PR events

codex_review_fixes.finding: HTTPS reachability checks probed the bare host root instead of /rest/v1/
codex_review_fixes.fix: Steps 3-4 probe https://$REST_HOST/rest/v1/ on host (IPv4/IPv6) and inside the worker container

codex_review_fixes.finding: worker.startup log line read via grep -m1 (oldest match), misreporting stale target after multiple restarts
codex_review_fixes.fix: Step 10 uses `grep worker.startup | tail -1` for the most recent line

codex_review_fixes.finding: Restart predicate (`claim_next_outbox failed\|502: Bad gateway`) let a non-502 claim failure (e.g. statement-timeout) authorize a production restart, and scanned an unbounded 'last 200 lines' rather than a bounded recent time window
codex_review_fixes.fix: Predicate now requires `claim_next_outbox failed:.*(502[^0-9]|bad gateway)` on the SAME line, within `docker logs --since 15m` -- a bounded, recent, 502-specific window

codex_review_fixes.finding: A failed `docker restart` or a container that never returned to running could still leave the workflow run green, since `set +e` suppressed the script's own exit code
codex_review_fixes.fix: Removed the blanket `set +e`; the restart exit code and every post-restart verification (running status, unchanged image, unchanged configured target, RestartCount advanced by exactly 1) now set an explicit FAILED flag whose value is the script's real exit code, propagated to the job via PIPESTATUS

codex_review_fixes.finding: Production Compose defines a Docker healthcheck for the worker, but the workflow treated Status=running alone as success -- an unhealthy worker could be reported as a successful recovery
codex_review_fixes.fix: Pre/post state now also captures .State.Health.Status. The wait loop polls both Status and Health for up to 60s. Post-restart verification requires Status=running AND (Health=healthy OR Health=none for containers with no healthcheck); Health=unhealthy or Health=starting-past-the-window both FAIL closed with an explicit error. Status=running is never labeled healthy anywhere in code, comments, evidence, or acceptance criteria.

codex_review_fixes.finding: Configured worker target was compared as the raw docker-logs line text (PRE_TARGET_LINE = POST_TARGET_LINE), which could false-fail on unrelated log metadata differences even when the actual target is unchanged
codex_review_fixes.fix: Both pre and post captures now parse the worker.startup JSON line with python3 and compare only the `targets` field's canonical JSON serialization; a JSON parse failure on either side also fails closed rather than comparing empty/garbage strings as equal

runtime_proof.note: Live smoke run per T1 policy; this lane changes only GitHub Actions workflow files and its own proof bundle, no production code path or migration.

queries.description: INSERT via processSubmission — one row per synthetic pick, in the 6 of 7 database-smoke tests that create a pick
INSERT via processSubmission (same 6 tests); UPDATE lifecycle_state via transitionPickLifecycle (queued, posted) and via the settlement RPC path

INSERT on each successful transitionPickLifecycle call and on each successful settlement transition; SELECT COUNT(*) used by three tests to assert zero rows were written for a specific to_state after a rejected atomic operation

INSERT via one successful enqueueDistributionAtomic call; UPDATE status to 'processing' via claimNextAtomic; a separate enqueue attempt is rejected and asserted via findByIdempotencyKey returning null
SELECT COUNT(*) used to assert zero rows were written after a rejected confirmDeliveryAtomic call

INSERT via recordPickSettlement/settlePickAtomic on successful calls; one settlePickAtomic call is rejected and asserted via SELECT COUNT(*) returning zero; SELECT used to assert exactly one base record and that a correction does not mutate the original row
SELECT COUNT(*) used by three tests to assert zero audit rows for a rejected atomic operation, and by one test to assert at least two audit rows exist for a successful settlement plus its correction
read-only SELECT (listByType('player')) scanning the full live table for the duplicate (external_id, sport, participant_type) invariant

known_limitations: ops-worker-recovery.yml is manual-dispatch-only and has NOT been dispatched by this lane -- PM review is required before any dispatch, per the issue's explicit instruction not to restart production yet.

The 15-minute abort-check window is a deliberate tradeoff: narrow enough that stale/historical log text cannot authorize a current restart, wide enough to catch a condition that was present when the operator decided to dispatch this workflow moments earlier.

acceptance_criteria.db_pooler_endpoint_tested: PASS -- step 6, TCP-connect only, no credentials
acceptance_criteria.host_firewall_egress_state_captured: PASS (best-effort, non-root SSH user may lack sudo) -- step 9
acceptance_criteria.worker_actual_configured_target_confirme: PASS -- step 10, reads the live worker.startup log line rather than assuming discord:canary/discord:best-bets
acceptance_criteria.db_pooler_variable_name_discovered_by_pa: PASS -- step 0 pattern-matches *DB*URL*/*POOL*/*CONNECTION* key NAMES only, falls back to the legacy fixed list only if no pattern match
acceptance_criteria.in_container_probe_works_when_curl_absen: PASS -- step 4 falls back to a Node https.request GET with no authorization header when curl is not on the worker image's PATH
acceptance_criteria.no_production_claim_rpc_invoked_with_rea: PASS -- both the curl and Node fallback probes are unauthenticated GET requests against the same route; no Authorization/apikey header is ever sent
acceptance_criteria.narrow_worker_only_recovery_workflow_add: PASS -- ops-worker-recovery.yml touches unit-talk-worker-1 only, workflow_dispatch-only with an explicit typed confirmation input
acceptance_criteria.recovery_workflow_no_env_target_deploy_p: PASS -- the only mutating command in the entire file is a single `docker restart unit-talk-worker-1`
acceptance_criteria.recovery_workflow_captures_pre_post_stat: PASS -- image/restart-count/target/health/logs captured both before and after
acceptance_criteria.recovery_workflow_requires_actual_502_no: PASS -- predicate is `claim_next_outbox failed:.*(502[^0-9]|bad gateway)` on the same line; a statement-timeout or other non-502 claim failure does not match
acceptance_criteria.recovery_workflow_bounded_time_window_no: PASS -- `docker logs --since 15m`, not an unbounded tail
acceptance_criteria.recovery_workflow_propagates_restart_fai: PASS -- nonzero `docker restart` exit sets FAILED=1
acceptance_criteria.recovery_workflow_requires_running_and_h: PASS -- since the production worker has a configured Docker healthcheck, success requires BOTH Status=running AND (Health=healthy OR Health=none for containers with no healthcheck); Health=unhealthy or Health=starting-past-the-60s-window both FAIL closed -- Status=running alone is never treated as success in code, comments, evidence, or acceptance criteria
acceptance_criteria.recovery_workflow_requires_unchanged_ima: PASS -- FAILED=1 if pre/post image differ; target compared via the canonically-parsed JSON `targets` field (not the raw log line, so timestamps/unrelated log metadata cannot cause a false failure)
acceptance_criteria.recovery_workflow_proves_exactly_one_res: PASS -- FAILED=1 unless post RestartCount == pre RestartCount + 1 exactly
acceptance_criteria.recovery_workflow_fails_red_on_any_unver: PASS -- `set +e` removed; FAILED is propagated as the real step/job exit code via PIPESTATUS, so GitHub Actions shows red on any of the above failures
acceptance_criteria.recovery_workflow_one_restart_attempt_ma: PASS -- exactly one `docker restart` call in the whole script, no retry loop around it
acceptance_criteria.recovery_workflow_not_dispatched_by_this: PASS -- prepared and committed only; returned for PM review before any dispatch
acceptance_criteria.current_pr_state_separated_from_prior_me: PASS -- prior_merged_history is now a dedicated, clearly-labeled sub-object; all top-level status/sha/ci fields describe PR #1258's own pre-merge state only
acceptance_criteria.regression_static_assertions_cover_the_s: PASS -- scripts/ops/worker-recovery-workflow.test.ts (12 tests, all passing) statically asserts against the checked-in script text: running+unhealthy=>FAIL, running+starting-past-timeout=>FAIL, running+healthy(or none)=>PASS is the only passing branch, changed-image=>FAIL, changed-target(canonical JSON comparison)=>FAIL, RestartCount-not-exactly-+1=>FAIL -- plus predicate/trigger/mutation-surface/set+e-removal coverage

owner_boundary.note: This is PR #1258's OWN merge authority state -- absent and pending, distinct from PR #1256's already-satisfied authority recorded in prior_merged_history above. Requires a fresh exact-head PM_VERDICT and t1-approved label bound to this PR's own head before merge.
